commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f14e97c7626b68d64e2dfd241a587b99a3c47fd0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds gateway session display/search fallbacks from configured channel account names for direct session rows, with focused session-utils tests.

PR surface: Source +157, Tests +109. Total +266 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can seed a direct Feishu session whose origin.label is the peer id and account name is configured, then buildGatewaySessionRow returns origin.label because no account-name lookup exists. I did not execute a live Feishu/OpenClaw runtime in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor action for a code fix plus real behavior proof; automation should not take over an external PR whose proof gate is unsatisfied.

target.title: fix(sessions): use account names for direct display
target.body:
## Summary

- Use configured channel account names as the display fallback for direct session rows when the current label is only an opaque direct peer id.
- Preserve existing precedence for explicit `displayName`, session labels, group display names, and meaningful origin labels.
- Include the account-name fallback in `sessions.list` search so users can find the readable account name.

Closes #79183

## Verification

- `git diff --no-index --check src/gateway/session-utils.ts.upstream src/gateway/session-utils.ts`
- `git diff --no-index --check src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts.upstream src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- `git diff --no-index --check src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts.upstream src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts`

Not run: the focused repo vitest command could not be run locally because repeated `git clone` attempts disconnected during fetch in this environment. The patch is based on current `main` raw files and includes focused regression coverage for `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts` and `src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Direct Feishu-style session rows can show the configured channel account name instead of an opaque direct peer id when no stronger label exists.

Real environment tested: API-authored fork branch from current upstream `main` raw files. A full local OpenClaw checkout was attempted repeatedly, but repository fetch disconnected in this environment before dependencies and a live app could be started.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --no-index --check src/gateway/session-utils.ts.upstream src/gateway/session-utils.ts`
- `git diff --no-index --check src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts.upstream src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- `git diff --no-index --check src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts.upstream src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts`

Evidence after fix:

- `src/gateway/session-utils.ts` now parses direct channel session keys, treats origin labels matching direct peer ids as technical fallback labels, and resolves `channels.<channel>.accounts.<account>.name` before falling back to the opaque id.
- `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts` adds account-name display fallback coverage while preserving explicit label precedence.
- `src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts` adds search-by-account-name coverage.

Observed result after fix: The added regression expectations assert `Quote Assistant` is returned/displayed for `agent:quote:feishu:direct:ou_8ad348410b`, while explicit labels and meaningful origin labels still win.

What was not tested: Focused vitest and a live Feishu/OpenClaw runtime were not run because this environment could not complete the repository checkout.
target.author: leno23
